Defying the Odds: Joaquim Lechà’s Journey from Athlete to Leading a Tech Powerhouse

From the adrenaline of competitive jet-skiing to steering one of Europe’s fastest-growing tech companies, Joaquim Lecha has always pushed boundaries. As the CEO of Typeform, Joaquim has taken lessons from his early days as an athlete - resilience, risk management, and boldness - and applied them to the business world.

An accident in his youth may have shifted his path, but it also ignited a drive that has propelled Typeform to a growth trajectory typically seen by U.S. tech giants. In an industry where European software companies often lag behind, Joaquim is setting a new standard, proving that with the right leadership, European companies can grow and scale at a global pace.

In our latest Leaders’ Lives interview, Joaquim reveals the crucial skill he learned as a one-man consultancy firm that has been instrumental in his success. Don’t miss out on this deep dive into the life and leadership of a CEO who is defying the statistics and leading by example.

text:
Think about the number of times you’ve taken a survey, rating your experience from 1 to 10. Has anything ever changed for you as a customer?

Too often the answer is ‘no.’ Companies are constantly collecting data, only for customers to never feel the impact.

The thing is, however, technology is rapidly changing – providing businesses with new ways to A) get better, richer data from customers that they can act on, while B) giving their customers a more engaging, personalized experience.

I recently had the opportunity to discuss this topic with Ton Dobbe 🔆 at South Summit. A few points from our conversation:

➡️ Structured data is easier to process, but misses an important aspect of human communication. "1 to 10” loses a lot of depth and meaning of that information. Someone can answer “6”, but we don’t know why.
➡️ Real language is more insightful – but it's challenging to process unstructured data at scale. Technologies historically haven’t been there, but AI is changing that, and it's now more available, faster, and cheaper to use.
➡️ Truly knowing your customers, and providing a unique personalized experience, is possible today – and you should be thinking about it both as something that is possible, and something that needs to be done. Don’t just ask for a number. Ask the question.

Thank you to Ton, and to all those who helped make South Summit a success. It’s moments like these, when we come together to share ideas, that help us collectively redefine customer experience.

profile.occupation: SaaS scaleups don’t fail because they don’t grow. They fail because they don’t grow enough. I remove the blocker hiding in plain sight. | Author of The Remarkable Effect

comments.text:
You're hitting a key point here, Joaquim. It's not about doing a survey. It's about what you do with it.

It's easy to pat yourself on the back and think you're doing a good job because the NPS scores went up, but the real value is in understanding WHY they went up (or vice versa). 

You can't get that from numbers. You only get that from genuine answers. 
That's where the true emotions hide.

We're at the start of an era where technology can finally help people make a far larger and more meaningful impact is possible. That's not about automation. It's about augmentation.

It was inspiring to hear how Typeform is riding that wave.

article.description: History teaches us that most worthwhile human endeavors require the strength of a unified team, because the team elevates every individual. Consider t

text:
I’m excited to share an article that provides a deeper look into Typeform's latest growth strategy.

Over the past year, we’ve been solving for the problem of fragmentation – separate teams, funnels, processes, and data – with a solution we call One Funnel. It’s our approach to unifying our GTM, so that we can create a comprehensive customer picture that empowers us to put every customer on the right journey.

We've recently been introducing One Funnel at various events – SaaSOpen, SaaStock, CRO Summit, YPO Summit – and it’s been inspiring to hear how other companies are approaching evolving their GTM, as we collectively look to achieve ambitious growth goals. 

I welcome you to share via the comments how you’re thinking about it, and we can continue the conversation.

text:
Finally a Founder in AI (yay!), working with the best team to build agentic products that will completely change how people engage and buy software.

As a student I saw the emergence of Internet, and my profession has allowed me to be part and see the impact of great technological transformations like internet, mobile telecoms, mobile data and SaaS. Each has reshaped the world in profound ways, but AI will surpass them all.

I learned the excitement and challenges of hypergrowth as an executive in start-up companies like Socialpoint and Typeform, that scaled from ~$15m to $100m+ in a few years. Now, with Supersonik, I get to start from zero, shaping everything from the ground up.

To me this is more than just building another successful company, it's about redefining how people everywhere in the world engage with software. And we're only just getting started!  

Experience our live AI Agent Demo, link in the comments!

This journey has taught us so much already. To all the founders out there: we are living in the fastest moving times in history, everything is possible. Whether you are from Barcelona, like we are, or from any other place in the world, it has never been easier to build globally and have an impact. Dream it, build it, ship it. With his deep expertise in product innovation and a customer-first mindset, Shaun is perfectly positioned to lead us through the next exciting phase of growth and innovation. We couldn’t be more excited about what’s to come!

Shaun’s vision for Lodgify is centered on further accelerating our growth to empower more vacation rental hosts with cutting-edge tools. Under his leadership, we will continue scaling our market presence, enhancing our product offerings, and strengthening our position as the most trusted and beloved platform for independent hosts worldwide.

Shaun brings a wealth of experience from leadership roles in the SaaS and tech sectors, including his most recent position as Chief Product Officer at Pipedrive.

As part of this transition, Dennis Klett, our co-founder and former CEO, will continue to support Lodgify’s long-term strategy as a non-executive board member.

We’re also excited to welcome Joaquim Lecha, former CEO of Typeform, as Chairman of the Board.

Welcome to the team, Shaun! We are excited for this new chapter of growth ahead. 

Read more here: https://lnkd.in/d_cMV5Zb

article.title: Why Every Leadership Team Needs a Captain of Staff

resharedPost.text:
I had my first time at Typeform…
My first experience as a Chief of Staff was at Typeform, working alongside CEO Joaquim Lecha. 

Here, I truly understood what it means to be the Captain of Staff 🫡.

Four lessons stood out:
1️⃣ Team Is Above the Individual ✨
Every decision had to prioritize the success of the team over personal goals. Aligning everyone behind shared objectives was non-negotiable.

2️⃣ Lead by Doing, Not Just Saying 💪
I learned the power of being the first to take action—modeling the behavior and energy I wanted to spread throughout the organization.

3️⃣ Building the Right Operating System 📊
Success wasn't just about working harder; it was about building systems and processes that allowed the team to execute effectively and scale sustainably.

4️⃣ It is about culture and Leadership 🌟
I discovered that great teams execute well when they operate on shared values and purpose. Leadership is about defining, reinforcing, and role-modeling the culture every day.

This experience taught me that leadership isn't about titles—it's about action, accountability, and building a foundation for others to thrive.

➡️ Curious to know how a Captain of Staff 🫡 can be your game-changer? Read more (https://lnkd.in/d6maTH6u)

💬 Or, does your leadership team already have its captain? Let me know in the comments! 👇

text:
Culture Change Starts with Leadership. 
I couldn’t agree more with Òscar Carbonell Dolz's post about the importance of Culture and Leadership. 
Last week, during a dinner with Òscar Carbonell Dolz, we had one of those deep, candid conversations about what it really takes to drive culture change in organizations. 
The key takeaway? Leadership is the foundation of any culture transformation. 
At dinner, we explored the critical elements that leaders must embrace to spark and sustain culture change. These four stood out: 
1) Awareness: Leaders need to be deeply aware of their strenghts, their motivations and, more importantly, their fears—what holds them back. 
2) Willingness to Change and Learn: Transformation requires stepping out of comfort zones, confront hard truths with honesty and learn throughout the process. 
3) Readiness: Having the right tools, support and circumstances is essential. Change isn’t just about intention—it’s about creating the conditions for success. 
4) Commitment to Persevere: Culture change is one of the hardest and most common challenges leaders face. It demands authenticity, trust and a clarity of where your are going.
This is what I learned from Òscar Carbonell Dolz, currently co-founder of LeaderNess and why I believe a #CaptainofStaff can make all the difference. Organizations can only move as far as their leaders are willing to lead themselves. The #CaptainofStaff becomes a critical partner in driving this transformation—by creating alignment, fostering accountability, and building the systems needed for sustainable change. 
I was lucky to have Òscar Carbonell Dolz as my partner in this journey. #Leadership #CultureChange #SelfAwareness #Commitment #Growth #CaptainOfStaff

text:
Hello! :)

A bit more than 6 years ago David and Robert gave me the opportunity to lead Typeform, and it is now the right time for me to also step aside and let a new leader take the helm.

Looking back on these last six years, I’m proud of what the team has achieved. Typeform is today a very profitable and cash generating company, having grown our customer base 3.6x to more than 150k worldwide and our ARR by 5x, a scale that few startups ever reach. 

Getting here hasn’t always been easy. Along the way, we have had moments of great uncertainty, and moments where failure was simply not an option, and plenty of moments where no matter what we did, it was going to be hard. 

And for navigating through all of these moments with me, I want to thank the many excellent professionals in Typeform and Typeform’s Board for making this possible. Thank you for your talent, for your support, effort and commitment to build a truly consequential Typeform that makes our customers the heroes of our story.

Over the last 18 months, with the launch of Typeform for Growth,  we’ve learned that product innovation is the key enabler to continue growing fast, and therefore, I’m very happy to hand over my position to Jay Choi.

I’ve known Jay for more than a year now—we welcomed him to the board to be our product voice, with his deep expertise in building and scaling innovative SaaS products.

Since then, I’ve come to know him as not only a kind person, but also a thoughtful, experienced, accomplished, and results-driven professional who not only excels at product, but has deep business domain expertise, taking companies to hundreds of millions of ARR.

My immediate priority is to assist Jay through this transition as an advisor to the CEO, while I start preparing for my next professional adventure!

article.title: Typeform Launches ‘Formless’: The AI-Powered Form Builder That Simulates Real Conversations

text:
What if forms were just a conversation? 

It’s this idea that sparked the inspiration for Typeform in 2012, and it’s also the idea that fueled the invention of Formless, our AI-powered form builder that not only asks, but also answers.

I’m excited to announce that as of today, Formless is publicly available: https://lnkd.in/eNNJbD5P 

Neither of these incredible products would exist without the vision, creativity, and builder mentality of David Okuniev. It’s bittersweet news to share that now after the release of Formless, David has decided it’s the right time to pursue innovation beyond data collection.

I want to thank him on behalf of the company for the many contributions over the last 12 years, and I also want to personally express my gratitude. David, you are one of the reasons I joined Typeform. You’ve already invented a product used and loved by millions of people around the world – I can’t wait to see what you do next.  

David will remain with Typeform as a board member, and in his own words, “There is so much more Typeform can and will do.” 

Please join me in celebrating the launch of Formless and in wishing David well on his future endeavors.
